entities.name: Agency
entities.role: A staffing supplier (recruitment agency) that provides workers to clients; tracked with priority, contact, pricing status, and activation date.

transitions.note: Date Activated column and 'ACTIVATE' string in source indicate this transition exists, but no explicit activate control or invite-to-active flow screen was observed.
Pricing column shows 'Pending N lines' and 'Invite Sent' date; 'Copy email' interaction (011) shows invite dispatch.
One agency (Staffline Group) shows Pricing = 'Applied 9 lines', distinguishing it from 'Pending'.
VMS platforms routinely allow suspending a supplier to halt new bookings without full removal; no such control found in any screen.
Counterpart to suspend; expected wherever suspension exists.
VMS systems require a formal offboarding step to end an agency relationship and prevent new assignments.

capabilities.name: List and search agencies
capabilities.note: Full paginated list with Search field, Priority, Agency Name, Contact, Email, Pricing, Invite Sent, Date Activated columns.

capabilities.name: View agency detail / profile
capabilities.note: Rate Simulator references 'Agencies → Staffline Group → Rate Configuration', implying a detail view exists, but no agency detail screen was walked. No explicit read/detail screen in evidence.

capabilities.name: Create agency
capabilities.note: VMS requires adding new supplier/agency records; no 'Add agency' or creation flow was observed in any screen.

capabilities.name: Edit agency profile
capabilities.note: Contact, priority, and other fields visible in the list would normally be editable in a detail view; no edit control or form was observed.

capabilities.name: Delete agency
capabilities.note: VMS systems typically allow removal of agencies that were added in error before they become active; no delete control found.

capabilities.name: Archive agency
capabilities.note: VMS platforms archive offboarded agencies to preserve historical rate and booking data while removing them from active selection.

capabilities.name: Send pricing invite
capabilities.note: Invite Sent date column and 'Copy email' interaction confirm the invite dispatch action is supported.

capabilities.name: Activate agency
capabilities.note: 'ACTIVATE' string found in source and 'Date Activated' column shown; however the actual activation control or flow screen was not walked.

capabilities.name: Suspend agency
capabilities.note: VMS platforms commonly allow suspending a supplier to pause new bookings while retaining the agency record.

capabilities.name: Offboard agency
capabilities.note: VMS requires a formal offboarding step to end an agency relationship and prevent further assignments.

capabilities.name: Configure agency rate / pricing
capabilities.note: Rate Simulator text states 'On-costs and margin are read from this agency's engine settings (Agencies → Staffline Group → Rate Configuration)' and source contains 'Agency Rate Config' and 'Agency config' strings, confirming configuration exists. However the rate configuration screen itself was not walked.

capabilities.name: Set agency priority
capabilities.note: A Priority column is visible in the list (all showing N/A), suggesting priority assignment is a feature, but no control or edit flow for setting priority was observed.

capabilities.name: Sort agencies by date
capabilities.note: Explicit 'Date Sent' and 'Date Activated' sort buttons are visible in the toolbar above the agency list.

entities.name: Position
entities.role: A named job role (e.g. FLT Driver, HGV Class 1, Picker/Packer) grouped under a job type, available for requisitions and referenced as the primary key in rate cards.

capabilities.name: List and search positions
capabilities.note: List is grouped by job type with a count summary (13 positions, 3 job types). Search input is visible.

capabilities.name: View position detail
capabilities.note: No detail/drill-down screen for an individual position is evidenced. A VMS typically shows a position detail page listing associated rate lines, linked requisitions, and accreditation requirements.

capabilities.name: Edit / rename position
capabilities.note: The positions list in 019_positions.txt shows no edit affordance (no edit icon, inline edit, or context menu). A VMS must allow renaming a position to correct labels without breaking rate-card references.

capabilities.name: Edit / rename job type
capabilities.note: Job types (Warehouse, HGV Driver, Van Driver) appear as grouping headers with no edit control visible. Renaming or re-grouping job types is a standard catalogue management operation.

capabilities.name: Delete position
capabilities.note: No delete affordance is visible. A VMS should prevent deletion of positions referenced by active rate lines or open requisitions, and allow deletion of unused positions.

capabilities.name: Delete job type
capabilities.note: No delete affordance for job types is shown. Required for catalogue housekeeping when a job category is retired.

capabilities.name: Use position as rate card lookup key
capabilities.note: Position is the primary, always-first lookup key in the Rate Simulator and is locked as the mandatory first dimension in the Rate Engine. Source confirms: 'Position is always the first key and can’t be changed'.

capabilities.name: Map / validate position against rate card upload
capabilities.note: Source (003_85d58d55.js) shows a validation error 'Unknown position / role not in catalogue' with the fix directing users to Settings → Positions, confirming the catalogue is used as the authoritative reference during rate card upload.

capabilities.name: Deactivate / disable position
capabilities.note: A VMS position catalogue must support deactivating positions that are no longer offered (e.g. seasonal roles retired) without deleting historical rate and booking data.

entities.name: Job Type
entities.role: A category grouping one or more positions (e.g. Warehouse, HGV Driver, Van Driver), drawn from rate cards and used to organise positions and segment rate lookups.

capabilities.name: Create job type
capabilities.note: The 'Add job type' button is visible in the Positions page UI. No modal or confirmation flow is shown in the walk evidence; button presence confirms the intent but the creation form is not captured.

capabilities.name: List / search job types
capabilities.note: Job types are listed as group headers in the Positions table (019) and as a filterable dropdown in the Rate Simulator (025). Source also exposes them as filter selects across multiple views.

capabilities.name: Read job type detail
capabilities.note: No dedicated job type detail screen is evidenced. In a VMS, viewing a job type's member positions, parity rules, and associated rate lines is a standard configuration read.

capabilities.name: Rename / update job type
capabilities.note: No edit affordance for an existing job type name or properties is shown. VMS configuration objects must be renameable to accommodate client-specific terminology changes without breaking rate lookups.

capabilities.name: Delete job type
capabilities.note: No delete affordance is evidenced. Standard VMS admin capability: removing a job type when no positions or rate lines are still associated.

capabilities.name: Archive / deactivate job type
capabilities.note: No deactivate or archive control is shown. VMS platforms commonly allow soft-deactivation of a job type so it stops appearing in requisition and rate-lookup dropdowns without permanently deleting it or its historical rate lines.

capabilities.name: Map unrecognised job type (from upload)
capabilities.note: Source (011_0153cc9b.js) shows validation that flags an unmatched job type from an uploaded rate card and renders a 'Map job type to …' dropdown inline on the validate-upload screen, enabling the user to resolve it without modifying the file.

capabilities.name: Filter rate card / rate matrix by job type
capabilities.note: Job type is a first-class filter dimension across the Rate Simulator, Rate Cards view, and positions grouping.

entities.name: Rate Card Upload
entities.role: A background file upload of a rate card spreadsheet that moves through states (uploading, ready to validate, validating, validated, applied/live) before becoming the active rate card.

capabilities.name: Download validation error report (CSV)
capabilities.note: Source (003_85d58d55.js) shows data-ru-csv button and downloadCsv function; rendered screen shows the validate UI context.

capabilities.name: Dismiss failed upload
capabilities.note: Source (003_85d58d55.js) shows data-ru-remove handler with 'Dismiss' button for failed status uploads.

capabilities.name: Discard a pending/invalid upload
capabilities.note: VMS upload queues commonly allow users to explicitly discard an upload that is ready-to-validate or has errors, to keep the queue tidy without going through apply. Only failed uploads expose a dismiss; non-failed uploads in the queue have no removal affordance.

capabilities.name: Continue to configuration (set effective date / mapping)
capabilities.note: Button 'Continue to configuration' is present in source (003_85d58d55.js, data-ru-configure) and rendered screen, but no configuration screen was captured in the walk — so the full flow (effective date setting, mapping resolution) is not confirmed.

capabilities.name: Search / filter uploads queue
capabilities.note: A search input is shown on the queue screen and an error-level filter exists in the validate screen (source: data-ru-sev buttons), but no full-queue filter controls beyond the search field are confirmed.

capabilities.name: Rollback / reactivate a prior version
capabilities.note: VMS systems commonly allow reverting the live rate card to a previously applied version when errors are discovered post-apply. The version history table is read-only — no reactivate or rollback action is visible.

capabilities.name: Schedule future effective date for upload
capabilities.note: VMS pricing systems commonly allow scheduling a rate card to go live at a future date (e.g. start of next pay period) rather than immediately. The 'Continue to configuration' step hints at this but no effective-date screen was captured.

capabilities.name: Notify stakeholders on apply
capabilities.note: Enterprise VMS platforms commonly trigger notifications (email or in-app) to affected agencies or internal users when a new rate card goes live, ensuring rate transparency. No notification affordance is visible.

entities.name: Pricing Rule
entities.role: A rule within the Rate Engine that defines one step in the pay/bill rate calculation (e.g. base rate, WTR holiday pay, employer NI, pension, markup), specifying method and level but not values.

capabilities.name: Create pricing rule
capabilities.note: Source confirms addRule() appends a draft rule to a group when the engine version is in draft state; 'Add rule' button visible per group.

capabilities.name: View pricing rule detail
capabilities.note: Rules expand to show type, calculation type, recipient, condition, bands, value level, and supplies. Rate Simulator renders each rule's computed contribution.

capabilities.name: List and search pricing rules
capabilities.note: Source confirms a live text search field ('Search rules…') filters the visible rule stack in real time.

capabilities.name: Edit pricing rule structure (type, calc, condition, level)
capabilities.note: Source shows inline dropdowns for Calculation type, Recipient, Condition, and Value level are editable on draft rules only. Rule name is set on creation but no explicit rename affordance is shown in the walk text; the source uses the initial 'New rule' name and a validation nudge, so rename is implied but not directly confirmed.

capabilities.name: Reorder pricing rules within a group
capabilities.note: Source shows a grip icon (ArrowsUpDownSmall) on each rule card indicating drag-to-reorder intent, and moveGroup() confirms group-level ordering; however no moveRule() function is present in source — rule-level reorder within a group is implied by the grip but not implemented in the prototype code.

capabilities.name: Delete pricing rule
capabilities.note: Source confirms delRule() removes a draft rule from its group; 'Remove rule' danger button rendered on draft rules in editable versions.

capabilities.name: Publish version (promotes all rules to live)
capabilities.note: publishVersion() sets the draft version to published and archives the previous published version; all contained rules become read-only.

capabilities.name: Create new engine version (branch rules for editing)
capabilities.note: Source confirms 'Create new version' clones the current published structure into a new draft version, preserving all existing rules for editing.

capabilities.name: Switch to existing draft version
capabilities.note: Source shows 'Switch to draft vX' button and supporting logic; walk confirms the read-only banner with that CTA on a published version.

capabilities.name: Restore / branch from archived version
capabilities.note: VMS rate engines commonly allow branching from a historical (archived) version to recover an earlier rule configuration; no affordance for this is present in the walk or source.

capabilities.name: Archive / deactivate pricing rule independently
capabilities.note: Individual rule archiving (soft-disabling a rule without deleting it, e.g. to preserve audit history) is expected in VMS engines that need to track when a rule was active; the prototype only supports delete or keeping the rule in a non-live version.

capabilities.name: Preview compiled rate-card schema (column impact of rules)
capabilities.note: Source renders a 'connected column preview' showing which columns each rule contributes to the rate-card import template; walk confirms the value-columns section adjacent to the pricing rules.

capabilities.name: Simulate rule output against a booking
capabilities.note: Rate Simulator screen shows each rule's computed £/hr contribution (base rate, WTR holiday pay, Employer NI, pension, apprenticeship levy, sick pay, markup) for a given position/agency/booking combination.

capabilities.name: Export / audit rule history
capabilities.note: VMS platforms commonly provide an export or audit log of rule changes across versions (who changed what, when) for compliance and client billing transparency; no such export affordance is evidenced.

entities.name: Rate Card
entities.role: A versioned file (xlsx/csv) containing pay and bill rate lines for every position, used as the live pricing source for rate calculations.

transitions.note: Apply button is present in the validate-upload flow (036_validate-upload.txt) but the draft state itself is not a named Rate Card version state — it belongs to the upload queue entity. The resulting version goes directly to active.
The version history table shows only one ACTIVE version; the prototype does not surface a superseded status on older Rate Card versions, only on Rate Engine versions.
No archive or retire action is present for a Rate Card version. The Rate Engine source (010_463be867.js) does model an 'archived' status but this applies to Rate Engine versions, not Rate Cards.

capabilities.name: View rate card version list
capabilities.note: Version history table with VERSION, STATUS, RATE CARD filename, EFFECTIVE date range, RATE LINES count, and APPLIED date is visible.

capabilities.name: View rate card version detail
capabilities.note: The version history table has selectable rows ('Select a version to open its details') but no detail screen is shown in the walk; content of a version detail view is unconfirmed.

capabilities.name: Search / filter version history
capabilities.note: A search field exists on the Rate Cards screen but it targets the uploads queue (by filename), not the version history table. No filter controls appear on the version history itself.

capabilities.name: Apply rate card (make version active)
capabilities.note: An 'Apply rate card' button is present once a validated upload has no blocking errors.

capabilities.name: Set effective date for rate card version
capabilities.note: The version history shows an 'EFFECTIVE' date range per version, and the source references an 'Effective date' column in the uploaded file. There is no UI control shown that lets an admin override the effective date separately from the file content.

capabilities.name: Download rate card template (blank schema)
capabilities.note: The Rate Engine screen (024_rate-engine.txt) exposes a 'Rate-card CSV template' download derived from the compiled schema; this is the template used to author a new rate card file.

capabilities.name: Re-upload corrected file
capabilities.note: A 'Re-upload corrected file' button is shown when a validated upload has errors, allowing replacement before applying.

capabilities.name: Archive / retire a rate card version
capabilities.note: VMS systems commonly allow explicit archiving of old rate card versions to distinguish intentionally retired cards from superseded ones; no such action appears in the prototype.

capabilities.name: Delete rate card version
capabilities.note: VMS pricing modules commonly allow deletion of erroneous or test rate card versions that were never made active; no delete control appears on any version history row.

capabilities.name: Export / download active rate card
capabilities.note: VMS users commonly need to export the current live rate card (e.g. for audit, agency distribution, or offline review); no export action is present for the active or historical rate card versions.

capabilities.name: Clear upload history
capabilities.note: A 'Clear history' button is visible on the uploads queue section of the Rate Cards screen.

capabilities.name: View rate card in rate simulator
capabilities.note: The Rate Simulator uses position/agency/location lookups that are sourced from the active rate card, providing an indirect read of live rate card values.

entities.name: Rate Engine
entities.role: A versioned configuration object that defines the structural rules (pricing rules and lookup keys) for how pay and bill rates are calculated — structure only, no values.

transitions.action: supersede on publish of newer draft (automatic)

capabilities.name: Create rate engine configuration (new legal entity)
capabilities.note: Source confirms 'Add legal entity' button in config menu, creating a new config starting as a draft (010_463be867.js).

capabilities.name: Edit pricing rules structure (draft only)
capabilities.note: Source confirms isEditable() guard, add/remove rules, reorder groups, edit rule fields — all gated to draft status.

capabilities.name: Edit rate card custom lookup keys (draft only)
capabilities.note: Rate Card custom Lookup section shown in 024; source confirms it is part of the editable draft structure.

capabilities.name: Delete / remove individual pricing rule
capabilities.note: Source shows 'Remove rule' button (data-re-delrule) gated to draft and non-locked rules.

capabilities.name: Delete entire rate engine configuration
capabilities.note: VMS commonly allows removal of a legal-entity pricing configuration when it is no longer needed; no delete/remove config affordance found in render or source.

capabilities.name: Archive a rate engine version
capabilities.note: Source shows 'archived' as an explicit status; previous published versions transition to archived when a new version is published.

capabilities.name: List / switch between rate engine configurations (per legal entity)
capabilities.note: Source shows config switcher menu listing all legal entity configurations.

capabilities.name: View version history and switch versions
capabilities.note: Source confirms version history menu with all versions listed; clicking switches the active view version.

capabilities.name: Create new version (copy from current)
capabilities.note: Render shows 'Create new version' button and 'Switch to draft v5' button; source confirms newVersion() copies structure into a new draft.

capabilities.name: Publish draft version (make live)
capabilities.note: Source shows 'Publish <label>' button in version menu; publishVersion() moves draft to published and previous published to archived.

capabilities.name: Switch active view to existing draft
capabilities.note: Render shows 'Switch to draft v5' button; source confirms data-re-switchdraft handler.

capabilities.name: Download rate card CSV template from engine
capabilities.note: Source confirms 'Download CSV template' button that emits value-free column schema derived from pricing rules and lookup keys.

capabilities.name: View compiled template overlay (fields and their kind)
capabilities.note: Source implements a 'View template' modal (C10) showing structure-only fields; download button inside modal.

capabilities.name: Preview/simulate rates against engine (rate simulator)
capabilities.note: Rate Simulator screen shows 'Master Rate Card v1 — the base pricing engine resolved from your Rate Cards' and applies engine rules to compute a live rate.

capabilities.name: Rename / relabel rate engine configuration
capabilities.note: VMS commonly allows renaming a legal-entity configuration; no rename affordance found in render or source beyond initial creation label.

capabilities.name: Export / import rate engine configuration
capabilities.note: VMS configurations are commonly exportable for backup or cross-environment migration; only the CSV template (value-free columns) is exportable, not the engine configuration itself.

entities.name: Rate Line
entities.role: A single pricing row within a rate card, specifying pay and bill rates for a given combination of position, shift type, location, and other lookup keys.

transitions.action: supersede via new rate card version

capabilities.name: Create rate line (via spreadsheet upload)
capabilities.note: Rate lines are created by uploading an xlsx/csv file; up to 100K+ lines per file. No in-UI manual row entry is shown.

capabilities.name: View rate line detail (rate waterfall breakdown)
capabilities.note: The rate simulator shows a full per-line waterfall: base pay, WTR holiday pay, employer NI, pension, apprenticeship levy, sick pay, direct cost, margin, and charge rate. Source confirms expandable detail rows per line in the preview screen.

capabilities.name: List and search rate lines
capabilities.note: Rate Cards screen shows a searchable table of rate lines with count, status, grouping by supplier/site/job type, sort controls, and filter chips (errors, missing margin, outliers, changed vs live).

capabilities.name: Edit rate line inputs (margin, pension, hours, sick pay, levy)
capabilities.note: Source confirms inline numeric editors for weekly hours, pension %, sick pay, margin, and levy toggle per line. Changes are recorded in an audit log. Net pay itself is read-only — must be corrected in the source file.

capabilities.name: Exclude rate line from staging
capabilities.note: An 'Exclude row' button is present on lines with acknowledgeable issues; excluded lines are removed from the staged set. This is the prototype's equivalent of soft-delete for a line in an upload.

capabilities.name: Archive / retire superseded rate lines
capabilities.note: A VMS commonly retains prior rate card versions for audit and historical billing lookups. There is no UI for browsing or explicitly archiving superseded rate lines from older versions — the version history shows only the active version row count.

capabilities.name: Validate rate lines (check NMW, duplicates, missing values, outliers)
capabilities.note: Validation screen shows per-line errors (below NMW, missing pay rate, bill < pay, NaN) and warnings (duplicate, outlier). Source enumerates all rule types and confirms live recomputation of charge rates.

capabilities.name: Apply rate card (transition staged lines to active/live)
capabilities.note: An 'Apply rate card' button is present once all blocking issues are resolved, transitioning the staged lines to the active version.

capabilities.name: Acknowledge warnings on a rate line
capabilities.note: Per-line 'Acknowledge & keep' button and bulk 'Acknowledge warnings' action are confirmed in source; allows warnings to be accepted without fixing the underlying value.

capabilities.name: Map unmatched site or job type on a rate line
capabilities.note: Inline dropdowns appear on lines with unmatched-site or unmatched-jt issues, letting the user map the uploaded column value to a known reference value.

capabilities.name: Compare rate lines across versions (diff view)
capabilities.note: A 'Compare changes' view in the rate preview screen shows new/changed/removed lines versus a selected baseline version, with delta £ and % per line.

capabilities.name: Simulate rate line in the rate calculator
capabilities.note: The Rate Simulator resolves a rate line for a given position/agency/location/shift, showing the full cost waterfall and total charge with adjustable assumptions.

capabilities.name: Export rate lines (download as CSV/XLSX)
capabilities.note: A downloadable CSV error report is confirmed in source for upload validation issues. No general export of all live rate lines as a spreadsheet is evidenced — only error-row export during validation.

capabilities.name: Bulk edit rate line inputs across multiple selected lines
capabilities.note: Bulk bar allows bulk levy on/off and bulk acknowledge across selected lines, but bulk editing of numeric fields (margin, pension) is not evidenced — only per-row inline editors are confirmed.

capabilities.name: View audit trail for rate line edits
capabilities.note: An audit log panel tracks every inline edit (margin, pension, hours, sick pay, levy, mappings) with timestamp, field, old/new value, and user name.
